author: Dr. Jennifer Adams
hpi: Mr. Morrison is a 73-year-old male with a complex medical history significant for HFrEF (EF 30-35%), CKD Stage 3b, Type 2 DM on insulin, persistent atrial fibrillation (off anticoagulation since GI hemorrhage 9/2023), diabetic peripheral neuropathy, HTN, hyperlipidemia, obesity, and BPH who presents for routine follow-up. He reports that his bilateral lower extremity edema has been mildly worse over the past 5-7 days, with his socks leaving deeper indentations than usual by evening. He attributes this to holiday dietary indiscretion in late December and early January, acknowledging that he consumed more sodium-rich foods including his wife Patricia's traditional holiday cooking. His daily home weights have trended from 97.0 kg in early January to 97.2-97.5 kg over the past week. He reports stable exertional dyspnea — he can walk approximately 2 blocks on flat ground before needing to rest, which is unchanged from his baseline over the past several months. He denies orthopnea, PND, or new-onset chest pain. He sleeps with two pillows as he has for the past year. His neuropathic foot pain remains at 4-5/10 on gabapentin, and he asks about pregabalin as an alternative. He completed his social work intake last week and reports his mood is slightly improved, though he still has difficulty motivating himself for activities he previously enjoyed.

reviewOfSystems.Constitutional: Reports persistent low energy and fatigue, worse in afternoons. Weight up 0.5 kg from last visit. Appetite somewhat decreased over past month. Denies fevers, chills, or night sweats.
reviewOfSystems.Cardiovascular: Mild bilateral leg swelling worse toward end of day. Stable exertional dyspnea at baseline. Denies orthopnea, PND, chest pain, or palpitations. No syncope or presyncope.
reviewOfSystems.Respiratory: Occasional dry cough, especially when lying down. No wheezing, hemoptysis, or pleuritic pain. No change in exercise tolerance beyond baseline.
reviewOfSystems.GI: Appetite fair but decreased. Regular bowel movements, no melena or hematochezia (monitored closely given GI bleed history). Taking pantoprazole daily. Mild occasional epigastric discomfort after large meals.
reviewOfSystems.GU: Nocturia x2-3, stable on tamsulosin. Good urinary stream. No dysuria, hematuria, or urgency. No genital infections since starting empagliflozin (May 2025).
reviewOfSystems.Musculoskeletal: Bilateral knee stiffness with prolonged sitting. No acute joint swelling or warmth. No gout flares since 2019.
reviewOfSystems.Neurologic: Bilateral foot numbness and burning at baseline (4-5/10). Tingling extends to mid-shin bilaterally. No new focal weakness, speech changes, or vision changes.
reviewOfSystems.Psychiatric: Mood slightly improved with counseling. PHQ-9 scored 8/27 at last assessment (January). Still has reduced interest in church and social activities. Sleep disrupted by nocturia and rumination. Denies SI/HI.
reviewOfSystems.Endocrine: Fasting blood sugars 130-170 per home monitoring. No episodes of hypoglycemia. No excessive thirst or polyuria beyond baseline.
reviewOfSystems.Skin: No new rashes. Feet examined daily by wife — no ulcers, blisters, or wounds. Dry skin on bilateral lower extremities.

physicalExam.General: Well-developed, obese male appearing stated age. Mildly fatigued-appearing but cooperative and engaged. No acute distress. Flat affect noted during portions of interview.
physicalExam.HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. PERRL 3mm bilaterally, brisk. Conjunctivae non-injected, sclerae anicteric. Oropharynx clear, moist mucous membranes. Dentition fair. No thyromegaly or lymphadenopathy.
physicalExam.Cardiovascular: Irregularly irregular rate at 64 bpm, consistent with atrial fibrillation. Normal S1. Physiologically split S2. Grade 2/6 systolic murmur at apex radiating to axilla, consistent with known mitral regurgitation. No S3 or S4 gallop. JVP estimated at 7 cm H2O (not elevated). PMI laterally displaced to 6th intercostal space, anterior axillary line.
physicalExam.Respiratory: Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally in all fields. No crackles, wheezes, or rhonchi. Good air movement. Symmetric chest expansion. No accessory muscle use.
physicalExam.Abdomen: Obese, soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly by percussion (liver span ~10 cm MCL). No splenomegaly. Normoactive bowel sounds in all four quadrants. No rebound or guarding. Small umbilical hernia palpable, easily reducible, non-tender (known, stable).
physicalExam.Extremities: Trace bilateral ankle edema (pitting, 1+), slightly more prominent on left than right. Warm and well-perfused. Bilateral dorsalis pedis pulses 1+ (diminished but palpable). Posterior tibial pulses 1+ bilaterally. No cyanosis or clubbing. Skin thin and dry on bilateral lower legs. No ulcers, calluses, or skin breakdown on feet.
physicalExam.Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3 (person, place, time). Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Motor strength 5/5 upper extremities, 4+/5 lower extremities (generalized, chronic). Decreased sensation to light touch and 10g monofilament bilateral feet extending to mid-shin. Vibration sense absent at bilateral great toes, diminished at ankles. Ankle reflexes absent bilaterally. Patellar reflexes 1+ bilaterally. Romberg positive with mild sway. Gait steady but cautious, narrow-based.
physicalExam.Skin: No rashes or lesions. Dry, scaly skin bilateral lower legs. No stasis dermatitis. No evidence of cellulitis.

assessment.diagnosis: Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction
assessment.notes: NYHA Class II, stable. EF 30-35% on last echo (9/2020, TEE 9/2024 confirmed). Currently on optimized quadruple GDMT: Entresto 97/103mg BID, carvedilol 25mg BID, spironolactone 25mg daily, empagliflozin 10mg daily. BNP 410 (stable from recent trend 380-460). Mild trace edema today, likely related to dietary sodium indiscretion over holidays. Volume status overall near euvolemic. ICD discussion remains open — patient has been deferring since 2022, meets criteria with EF <=35% on optimal GDMT >3 months.

assessment.diagnosis: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus with diabetic neuropathy
assessment.notes: A1c 7.4% — at goal for this patient given age, CKD, and hypoglycemia history (June 2020 event). On metformin 250mg BID (renally dosed), insulin glargine 24 units QHS, sliding scale lispro with meals, and empagliflozin 10mg daily. A1c has improved significantly since empagliflozin was added (was 7.8% in Oct 2024, now 7.4%). Mild NPDR on last eye exam (Dec 2024) — ophthalmology follow-up due in 6 months.

eGFR 35.4, Cr 1.96 — stable. Rate of decline has been ~2-3 mL/min/year. UACR 180 at last check (March 2024, due for recheck). On nephroprotective agents: Entresto, empagliflozin. K 4.8 — elevated but acceptable; monitoring closely given Entresto + spironolactone + empagliflozin combination. Sodium bicarbonate 650mg BID for metabolic acidosis (HCO3 improved to 24 from 22). Next nephrology follow-up scheduled.

Rate controlled on carvedilol 25mg BID (HR 64 today). CHA2DS2-VASc score 5 — annual stroke risk ~6.7% without anticoagulation. Warfarin permanently discontinued after GI hemorrhage (Sept 2023) per joint GI/Cardiology/PCP decision. On aspirin 81mg daily for modest stroke risk reduction. TEE (Sept 2024) showed no LAA thrombus. Patient counseled on stroke warning signs at each visit. Watchman device discussed but patient declines.

Bilateral, length-dependent sensorimotor neuropathy. Symptoms at 4-5/10 on gabapentin 300mg TID. Patient inquiring about alternatives — may consider pregabalin trial if gabapentin ceiling reached, though need renal dosing. Contributes to fall risk (one fall Sept 2025, no fracture).

PHQ-9 of 8/27 (mild). Began social work counseling in January 2026 — 4 sessions planned. Patient engaged but prefers to defer pharmacotherapy. Will reassess at next visit. Related to functional decline and chronic illness burden per wife's report.

plan.action: Continue optimized quadruple GDMT: Entresto 97/103mg BID, carvedilol 25mg BID, spironolactone 25mg daily, empagliflozin 10mg daily. Continue furosemide 60mg AM + 40mg PM (asymmetric dosing for nocturia). Reinforce daily weights — call if >3 lb gain in 24 hours. Reinforce sodium restriction <2g/day and fluid intake 1.5-2L/day. Trace edema today is mild — no diuretic adjustment needed at this time. Will re-address ICD discussion at cardiology follow-up. Recheck BNP with next labs.

Continue metformin 250mg BID, insulin glargine 24 units QHS, sliding scale lispro with meals, empagliflozin 10mg daily. A1c 7.4% at target — no medication changes. Continue home glucose monitoring BID. Reinforce carbohydrate-consistent diet. Ophthalmology follow-up for diabetic retinopathy surveillance in June 2026. Podiatry follow-up in 6 months for neuropathic foot care.

Continue nephroprotective agents (Entresto, empagliflozin). Continue sodium bicarbonate 650mg BID. Monitor BMP including potassium closely given K 4.8 on Entresto + spironolactone + empagliflozin. Recheck UACR (last done 3/2024). Avoid nephrotoxins — strict NSAID avoidance counseled. Renal dietitian follow-up ongoing. Next nephrology appointment to be scheduled.

Rate controlled on carvedilol (HR 64). No anticoagulation — continue aspirin 81mg daily per multidisciplinary decision (9/2023). Continue pantoprazole 40mg daily for GI protection. Stroke warning signs reviewed with patient and wife. No new neurological symptoms.

Continue gabapentin 300mg TID. Pain at 4-5/10, patient asking about pregabalin. Will discuss with neurology if symptoms worsen. Renally-dosed pregabalin would be 75mg BID if transition needed. Continue daily foot inspection by wife, protective footwear, custom orthotics.

Continue social work counseling — 2 of 4 sessions completed. PHQ-9 to be re-administered at next visit. If score >10 or symptoms worsening, will discuss SSRI (sertraline preferred given cardiac safety profile). Patient defers pharmacotherapy for now.
Metformin 250mg BID and gabapentin 300mg TID — 90-day supplies to Walgreens.

Labs: BMP, CBC, A1c, BNP, UACR — draw 1 week prior to next visit. Return to PCP in 6 weeks (approximately 3/10/2026). Annual cardiology visit upcoming. Nephrology follow-up to be scheduled. Ophthalmology follow-up June 2026.

attestation:
I personally saw and examined the patient. I reviewed the history, performed the physical examination, and developed the assessment and plan as documented above.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Jennifer Adams
Date: 1/27/2026
